channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Construction of Brisbane’s 2032 Olympic stadium begins amid protests over Victoria Park development
item.description: Construction has officially begun on the main stadium for the 2032 Olympic Games in Brisbane, following the removal of protestors from Victoria Park. The site, located near the city centre, was handed...

item.title: Faith Mangope: journalism rooted in adversity and a commitment to accountability
item.description: Faith Mangope, an acclaimed Metro FM broadcaster, credits her journey from poverty and hardship with shaping her fearless approach to journalism and her dedication to social equity. In an interview wi...

item.title: Saskatoon company launches fundraiser to support employees displaced by house fire
item.description: A Saskatoon business is rallying community support after two of its employees were affected by a house fire that caused significant damage and left them displaced. The company has organized a fundrais...

item.title: Sudbury lottery winner celebrates $2M prize and looks forward to sharing news with family
item.description: A Sudbury man is celebrating a life-changing lottery win of more than $2 million, expressing excitement and gratitude after discovering his newfound fortune. According to the report, the winner plans ...

item.title: DCI Arrests Missing Student in Connection with Utumishi Girls School Fire That Killed 16
item.description: In Gilgil, Nakuru County, a pre-dawn fire broke out at Utumishi Girls Senior Secondary School, tragically claiming the lives of 16 students who were asleep in their dormitory. The Directorate of Crimi...

item.title: Asian equities surge to record highs as AI optimism drives markets while oil prices rise on Middle East ceasefire uncertainty
item.description: Stock markets in Japan and South Korea reached fresh record highs on Monday, driven largely by strong investor enthusiasm surrounding artificial intelligence-related growth and solid corporate perform...

item.title: Elderly couple found fatally stabbed in Queens apartment; police continue investigation with no arrests
item.description: An elderly couple was found dead following a violent stabbing inside their apartment in the Fresh Meadows neighborhood of Queens, New York City, according to local reports. The victims, a 71-year-old ...

item.title: Alex Eala set to partner with Venus Williams in Bad Homburg Open doubles, report says
item.description: Filipina tennis player Alex Eala is reportedly set to join forces with American tennis icon Venus Williams for the upcoming Bad Homburg Open, according to a report by ABS-CBN News published on June 1,...

item.title: India becomes Australia’s largest source of migrants, signalling a major demographic and economic shift
item.description: The article discusses a significant demographic milestone in Australia, where India has, for the first time since records began, overtaken England as the country’s largest source of migrants. The shif...

item.title: The Long-Term Investment Opportunities of a Systemic Energy Transition
item.description: Global energy markets are undergoing a profound transformation that extends far beyond the shift from fossil fuels to renewables. Recent geopolitical tensions, supply chain challenges, and oil price v...

item.title: Dangote Keeps Top Spot as Africa’s Most Admired Brand for 8th Consecutive Year
item.description: Dangote Industries Limited don once again confirm say e still be Africa’s most admired African brand for the eighth year straight. This recognition come from the 16th annual Brand Africa 100 rankings ...

item.title: Dua Lipa and Callum Turner hold intimate London ceremony before Sicily celebrations
item.description: Dua Lipa, the 30-year-old pop star, and 36-year-old actor Callum Turner have officially married at an intimate ceremony at Old Marylebone Town Hall in London. Following this civil ceremony, the couple...

item.title: Challenges Mount as Trump Faces Political, Domestic, and International Pressure in Second Term
item.description: President Donald Trump is encountering significant challenges in his second term, with multiple fronts showing signs of stagnation. His efforts to broker a peace deal with Iran have yet to succeed, de...

item.title: Joseph-Aukuso Suaalii shines with powerful run despite Waratahs' narrow loss
item.description: After missing the majority of the 2026 Super Rugby season due to cross-code commitments, Joseph-Aukuso Suaalii made a striking return to the field for the Waratahs. During their closely contested matc...

item.title: Balancing caregiving for aging parents with retirement savings planning
item.description: The article explores the growing challenge many Canadians face when trying to support aging parents while still saving for their own retirement. It follows the example of Sophia and Carmen, a Montreal...

item.title: DCI identifies seven students linked to Utumishi Girls Academy dormitory fire through CCTV evidence
item.description: Kenyan newspapers on June 1 reported major developments in the ongoing investigation into the tragic Utumishi Girls Academy dormitory fire in Gilgil, which left 16 students dead and 79 others injured....

item.title: Ghana’s Black Stars Aim for Surprise in 2026 World Cup Group L
item.description: Ghana’s Black Stars are preparing to make a statement at the 2026 FIFA World Cup despite being the lowest-ranked African nation in the tournament. Currently ranked 74th in the world, the team has stru...

item.title: Tanzanian Health Experts Urge Preventive Care as NCD Costs Grow, Warning on NHIF Strain
item.description: Tanzania’s doctors are urging a major shift from treating illnesses to preventing them, arguing that non-communicable diseases (NCDs) now drive over half of the National Health Insurance Fund’s (NHIF)...

item.title: Decline in US student enrolment in India linked to rankings, curriculum rigidity and infrastructure concerns
item.description: The article reports a significant decline in the number of American students choosing India as a study-abroad destination over the past decade. According to data from the Institute of International Ed...

item.title: Nottingham maternity services face scrutiny over unsafe care, poor culture and systemic failures
item.description: A BBC Panorama investigation has revealed serious concerns about maternity services at Nottingham University Hospitals NHS Trust, where families were allegedly failed over more than a decade. The inqu...
